what_it_does: A daemon that turns OpenWebUI channels into a multi-room operator console. It signs into OWUI, maintains per-channel rooms and webhooks, spawns and tears down supervised AI members via a supervisor bridge, routes human and member messages, executes slash commands scoped to the current channel, handles native OWUI reaction teardown/interrupts, and exposes a local op-server so an AI operator member can translate plain-language requests into the same command set.
observations: State is a JSON file keyed by OWUI channel id: each room has a name and a roster of labelled members, and webhooks are stored per channel per label.
The daemon is the only holder of the OWUI token; the CLI is a thin client that POSTs to a local HTTP op-server.
Members are reached through cc_channels.push and registered/deregistered via cc_clone helpers from the runtime package.
Slash commands are parsed from message content and dispatched against the channel the message arrived in.
A dedicated operator member can be spawned; its system prompt tells it to run local CLI commands to perform ops.
Member-to-member @mentions are routed between room rosters, guarded by a per-room circuit breaker (8 hops in 90 seconds).
Reactions on member messages (🛑, ❌, ⏸, ✋) trigger teardown or interrupt via the supervisor.
Meta is kept string-only (from, thread) based on a noted prior bug where non-string meta broke channel surfacing.
The socket.io client joins channels on connect and listens to events:channel for messages and reactions.
standouts: Reaction-driven lifecycle control using native OWUI UI primitives.
An operator AI member that converts natural language into self-calls of the local op CLI.
Per-room member-to-member routing with a simple time-window circuit breaker.
Three simultaneous control surfaces (slash, local HTTP, natural language) backed by one dispatch table.
conventions: Environment-variable driven configuration with hardcoded fallback defaults.
Global mutable state protected by a single RLock.
Function-table dispatch (OPS dict) for slash/op commands.
Background daemon threads for message routing and the op-server.
Atomic state writes using a temporary file and os.replace.
Mixed REST and Socket.IO usage against OWUI and the supervisor.
concerns: Broad exception swallowing in many paths hides failure modes.
Reaction matching uses substring checks on emoji names, risking false positives.
The default HOME channel id is hardcoded in the file.
The CLI reads the state file to discover the op-server port, creating a potential race with daemon writes.
op_spawn_operator embeds the current interpreter path and script path into the persona, assuming repository layout.
_route_worker runs an infinite loop with no graceful shutdown mechanism.
No argument validation beyond catching exceptions inside dispatch.
notes: This file is the live control surface: it wires OWUI chat into a multi-agent room where Tim and AI members manage each other through chat, reactions, and a local op API.
questions: What is the exact request/response schema for the supervisor /bridge-session, /teardown, and /interrupt endpoints?
How do cc_channels.push and cc_clone register/deregister actually transport messages to supervised sessions?
What was the precise non-string meta failure that motivated the string-only meta rule?
How are OWUI channel ids preserved across restarts and reinstalls?
What happens when a user addresses the operator in natural language but no operator member has been spawned?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a multi-room operator console driven by slash commands, a local op endpoint, natural language via an operator member, and native OWUI reactions.
apparent_themes: Surface-native control using existing chat UI primitives
Credential centralization with least-privilege local op access
Loop prevention through per-room circuit breaking
String-typed metadata discipline for transport safety
intention_signals: Build a conversational, room-scoped command center where a human can spawn, pause, rename, and converse with AI members as if managing a team in a chat app, with the daemon acting as the single trusted executor.
novelty: The combination of reaction-based member lifecycle control, an operator member that issues CLI commands back to the same daemon, and per-room @mention routing with a circuit breaker is unusual for a chat-integrated agent console.
contribution: A concrete integration layer that binds OpenWebUI channels to supervised AI members, providing room-scoped slash commands, reaction controls, webhook management, inter-member routing, and a local credential-less op API for AI operators.
summary_for_embedding: ops/owui_room.py is a daemon that turns OpenWebUI channels into a multi-room operator console for managing and conversing with supervised AI members via slash commands, reactions, and a local op-server.
intention_for_embedding: To give a human a native chat-based control surface for spawning, pausing, tearing down, and talking to multiple AI members in scoped rooms.

symbols.does: Loads persisted room/webhook state and migrates older flat formats.
symbols.description: Reads the JSON state file, ensures rooms and webhooks dictionaries exist, migrates a pre-multi-room flat roster into the HOME room, and creates a default HOME room if absent.

symbols.does: Atomically persists current state to disk.
symbols.description: Creates the data directory if needed, writes state to a temporary file, then replaces the canonical state file to avoid partial writes.

symbols.does: Returns the member roster for a channel.
symbols.description: Looks up the room entry by channel id and returns its roster list, creating a default empty room record if the channel is not yet known.

symbols.does: Finds a room member by label.
symbols.description: Searches the roster of the given channel for a member whose label matches the supplied name, returning the member dict or None.

symbols.does: Authenticates with OWUI and returns a bearer token.
symbols.description: POSTs email and password from environment variables to OWUI's signin endpoint, raising an error if no password is set or the request fails.

symbols.does: Creates or reuses an OWUI webhook for a label in a channel.
symbols.description: Checks the cached webhooks for the channel and label, creates a new webhook via OWUI if missing, stores it in state, and returns the webhook record.

symbols.does: Builds the POST URL for a channel label's webhook.
symbols.description: Calls ensure_webhook and formats the OWUI webhook endpoint URL using the stored id and token.

symbols.does: Posts a message to a channel as a given label.
symbols.description: Uses the cached webhook for the label to POST content to OWUI, catching and printing any transport errors.

symbols.does: Creates a new OWUI channel.
symbols.description: POSTs a channel name to OWUI's channel creation endpoint and returns the created channel record.

symbols.does: Lists existing OWUI channels.
symbols.description: GETs the OWUI channels list and returns it, defaulting to an empty list if the response is empty.

symbols.does: Sends a POST request to the supervisor service.
symbols.description: Builds a JSON POST request to the configured supervisor base URL plus the given path and returns the parsed JSON response.

symbols.does: Returns the help text for operator commands.
symbols.description: Produces a formatted string listing available slash and natural-language commands and their usage.

symbols.does: Lists members in the current room with status.
symbols.description: Iterates the channel roster, resolves each member through cc_channels, reports transport and supervisor session state, and returns a formatted status string.

symbols.does: Fetches the state of a supervisor session.
symbols.description: GETs the supervisor sessions list and returns the state field for the matching session id, or absent markers if not found.

symbols.does: Spawns a new supervised member in the room.
symbols.description: Creates a supervisor bridge session with a persona that instructs the member how to speak via its webhook curl, registers the member with cc_clone, adds it to the roster, and saves state.

symbols.does: Removes a member from the room and tears down its session.
symbols.description: Finds the member, calls the supervisor teardown endpoint if it is supervised, deregisters it via cc_clone, removes it from the roster, and persists state.

symbols.does: Renames a member label in the room.
symbols.description: Updates the label field of the matching roster entry after checking the new label is not already taken, then saves state.

symbols.does: Re-briefs a member with a new role.
symbols.description: Queues a re-brief message to the member's handle so the supervisor session receives the new role description.

symbols.does: Sends a direct message to a member.
symbols.description: Queues the supplied text to the member's handle for delivery through the routing worker.

symbols.does: Creates a new OWUI channel and registers it as a live room.
symbols.description: Creates the channel, stores it in rooms with an empty roster, ensures an operator webhook, saves state, and re-emits the socket.io channel join so events arrive.

symbols.does: Lists OWUI channels and marks registered rooms.
symbols.description: Fetches the OWUI channel list and returns a formatted list, appending a room tag to channels already tracked in state.

symbols.does: Interrupts a supervised member's current turn.
symbols.description: Resolves the member's registration and POSTs an interrupt request to the supervisor for its session.

symbols.does: Interrupts all supervised members in the room.
symbols.description: Loops over the roster, finds supervised members with active supervisor sessions, and sends an interrupt request for each.

symbols.does: Returns a control-panel text summary.
symbols.description: Builds a formatted panel showing available reaction and slash controls for each member and for all members.

symbols.does: Returns combined room status.
symbols.description: Concatenates member status, channel list, and the current state of the member-to-member circuit breaker for the room.

symbols.does: Resets the member-to-member circuit breaker.
symbols.description: Clears the tripped flag and drops the recent-hop window for the channel, allowing member-to-member routing again.

symbols.does: Spawns the natural-language operator member.
symbols.description: Creates a supervised operator session whose persona instructs it to run local CLI commands against the op-server, registers it, and adds it to the roster.

symbols.does: Looks up and executes an operation by name.
symbols.description: Finds the operation in the OPS table, runs it under the global lock, and returns its string result or an error message.

symbols.does: Background thread that delivers queued messages to members.
symbols.description: Continuously pulls handle/content pairs from a queue and calls cc_channels.push with retries and logging, giving up after many attempts.

symbols.does: HTTP request handler for the local op-server.
symbols.description: Subclass of BaseHTTPRequestHandler that parses JSON op requests, dispatches them, posts the result back to the channel, and returns a JSON response.

symbols.does: Suppresses default access logging.
symbols.description: Overrides BaseHTTPRequestHandler.log_message with a no-op to keep daemon output quiet.

symbols.does: Handles POST /op on the local server.
symbols.description: Reads the JSON body, extracts op and args, resolves the channel, dispatches the operation, posts the result as the operator, and writes an HTTP JSON response.

symbols.does: Starts the local ThreadingHTTPServer for ops.
symbols.description: Binds to 127.0.0.1 on the configured op port, starts serve_forever in a daemon thread, and records the port in state.

symbols.does: Maps a webhook id to a member label in a channel.
symbols.description: Searches the cached webhooks for the given channel and returns the label whose webhook id matches.

symbols.does: Processes OWUI reaction events for member lifecycle control.
symbols.description: Identifies the member label from the reaction's webhook meta and, depending on the emoji, dispatches teardown or interrupt, posting the result as operator.

symbols.does: Routes a member's @mention to another member.
symbols.description: Parses the sender from webhook meta, detects @target at the start of the message, checks the circuit breaker, and queues delivery to the target member with reply instructions.

symbols.does: Daemon entry point.
symbols.description: Signs in, loads state, ensures webhooks for all known members and operators, starts the routing worker and op-server, connects the socket.io client, and waits for channel events.

symbols.does: Socket.io connect handler.
symbols.description: Emits user-join and join-channels events with the bearer token so the daemon receives events for its rooms.

symbols.does: Socket.io channel event handler.
symbols.description: Dispatches slash commands, natural-language operator messages, member-to-member routing, reaction handling, and broadcast delivery based on the incoming OWUI event payload.

symbols.does: Command-line client for the local op-server.
symbols.description: Parses arguments, discovers the op-server port from state, POSTs the operation, and prints the returned result.
